name: ContextMenu
description: A right-click (or long-press on touch) context menu, supporting items, checkbox items, radio items, submenus, and keyboard navigation. Built on Base UI ContextMenu with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Provides context menu state and context. Does not render a DOM element.

open.description: Whether the context menu is open (controlled).

onOpenChange.description: Callback when the context menu open state changes.

Trigger.description: The area that opens the context menu on right-click or long press. Renders a div, not a button.

dataAttributes.data-pressed: Present when the trigger has been activated.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.

Popup.description: The context menu popup container. Contains the menu items.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the menu is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the menu is closed.
dataAttributes.data-starting-style: Present on first render when opening for CSS transition.
dataAttributes.data-ending-style: Present when closing for CSS transition.

Item.description: A context menu item. Supports keyboard navigation and focus management.

destructive.description: Whether the item represents a destructive action, styled with destructive colors.

closeOnClick.description: Whether clicking the item closes the menu.

dataAttributes.data-highlighted: Present when the item is highlighted via keyboard or pointer.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

Separator.description: A visual separator between context menu items.

cssRequirements.description: Context menu popup enter/exit animations using Base UI data attributes.

intents.reasoning: ContextMenu is the primary component for right-click or long-press triggered menus. It handles the activation gesture, keyboard navigation, and ARIA attributes.

avoidWhen.scenario: Menu triggered by a visible button
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Menu instead. ContextMenu hides activation behind a right-click gesture that is not discoverable on its own.

avoidWhen.scenario: Selecting a single value from a list (form input)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Select or Combobox for form value selection. ContextMenu is for actions, not form values.

avoidWhen.scenario: The only way to access an action
avoidWhen.reasoning: Right-click is not discoverable. Always provide an alternative path (toolbar button, Menu) for any action exposed via ContextMenu.
avoidWhen.alternative: Toolbar or Menu in addition to ContextMenu

examples.name: with-submenu
examples.description: Context menu with a nested submenu
